factors.summary: The company has increased its borrowings, signaling potential confidence in growth but also indicating possible leverage.

evidence.text: Net short-term borrowings (repayments) primarily represent activity under our CP program and borrowings under the 364-Day Revolver. We primarily borrow under our CP program, as needed and availability allows.

factors.summary: Demand for Equifax's services is expected to experience modest growth in the United States in 2016, driven by economic activity and consumer credit growth.

evidence.text: In 2016, in the United States, we expect modest growth in overall economic activity and consumer credit for the remainder of the year.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is expected to range between 6% and 8% over the long term, driven by product innovation and business execution.

evidence.text: Over the long term, we expect that our ongoing investments in new product innovation, business execution, enterprise growth initiatives, technology infrastructure, and continuous process improvement will enable us to deliver long-term average organic revenue growth ranging between 6% and 8% with additional growth of 1% to 2% derived from strategic acquisitions consistent with our long term business strategy.

factors.summary: Transaction counts and volumes are growing across various segments, driven by higher average revenue per unit and increased volumes in key verticals.

evidence.text: Revenue for the first quarter of 2016 increased 5% when compared to the first quarter of 2015, driven by growth in our core credit decisioning products as well as our identity and fraud solutions products.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding, primarily due to strong revenue growth and cost management.

evidence.text: Operating margin increased from 26.9% in the first quarter of 2015 to 28.3% in the first quarter of 2016, due to strong revenue growth.
evidence.subsection: Workforce Solutions Operating Margin / Global Consumer Solutions

factors.summary: Management expects continued growth in revenue and earnings per share, with a focus on organic growth and strategic acquisitions.

evidence.text: Over the long term, we expect that our ongoing investments... will enable us to deliver long-term average organic revenue growth ranging between 6% and 8% with additional growth of 1% to 2% derived from strategic acquisitions.

factors.summary: The Veda acquisition has been integrated and is contributing to revenue growth, particularly in the Asia Pacific region.

evidence.text: On February 24, 2016, we completed the acquisition of Veda... We financed the cash portion of the purchase price through a combination of new debt... Refer to Note 5 for further information on debt.

factors.summary: The economic environment is a mixed tailwind, with modest growth in the U.S. but challenging conditions internationally due to political and fiscal issues.

evidence.text: In 2016, in the United States, we expect modest growth in overall economic activity and consumer credit for the remainder of the year. Internationally, the environment continues to be challenging as various countries address their particular political, fiscal and economic issues.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is strong, supporting reinvestment and strategic initiatives.

evidence.text: Management assesses liquidity in terms of our ability to generate cash to fund operating, investing and financing activities. We continue to generate substantial cash from operating activities and remain in a strong financial position, with resources available for reinvestment in existing businesses, strategic acquisitions and managing our capital structure to meet short- and long-term objectives.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated a positive outlook on future growth through increased capital expenditures. Both subsections highlight investments in new products, technology infrastructure, and acquisitions, indicating a commitment to expansion.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures in the first three months of 2016 increased by $18.6 million from the same period in 2015 as we are continuing to invest in new products and technology infrastructure.
Capital expenditures increased by $6.1 million in the first quarter of 2016, due to the Veda acquisition.

factors.summary: The company's shareholder returns have been maintained through consistent dividend payments, with no share repurchases in the first quarter of 2016, despite an increased dividend reflecting confidence in financial health.

evidence.text: We increased our quarterly dividend from $0.29 per share to $0.33 per share as announced in the first quarter of 2016.

We did not repurchase shares of our common stock during the first three months of 2016. At March 31, 2016, $ 667.2 million was available for future purchases of common stock under our share repurchase authorization. We paid out $39.2 million or $0.33 per share in dividends to our shareholders during the first three months of 2016.
